context: Evaluate a generated Python project Makefile for correct phony targets, variable use, and multi-step recipe handling

checklist.name: Modern header present
checklist.description: Makefile begins with SHELL := bash, .ONESHELL:, .SHELLFLAGS := -eu -o pipefail -c, .DELETE_ON_ERROR:, .SUFFIXES:, and both MAKEFLAGS += lines

checklist.name: PYTHON variable uses ?=
checklist.description: PYTHON is declared with ?= python3 so callers can substitute a different interpreter path

checklist.name: .PHONY covers all targets
checklist.description: develop, test, lint, build, clean, and help are all listed in .PHONY

checklist.name: develop target correctness
checklist.description: develop recipe installs in editable mode with dev extras using pip install -e .[dev] or equivalent; uses $(PYTHON) variable

checklist.name: clean target completeness
checklist.description: clean recipe removes dist/, build/, *.egg-info/, and __pycache__ directories; uses $(RM) -r or rm -rf idiom

checklist.name: Multi-step recipe safety
checklist.description: No recipe uses cd in one line and a command in the next separate line; commands are either chained with && or .ONESHELL: is active from the header

checklist.name: help target with ## comment extraction
checklist.description: Each user-facing target has a ## comment; help target extracts and prints them using grep or awk
